title: Colony Picking
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ie4e","text":"Isolate a single strain from frozen stock or colonies on culture media, then purify and establish subsequent liquid culture or frozen storage to ensure uniformity and reproducibility of experimental materials.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bbemq","text":"Frozen glycerol stock culture or colonies from LB agar plates","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8rqih","text":"LB broth (liquid medium)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"31brf","text":"LB agar plate (or species-specific medium, e.g., Pseudomonas isolation agar)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":49,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3fq33","text":"Sterile PBS (optional, if dilution is required)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5jc9i","text":"Sterile glycerol (for preparing glycerol stocks, final concentration 20%)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bbri7","text":"Sterile inoculation loop","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5n34h","text":"50 mL Falcon tube or other liquid culture vessel","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bfa1a","text":"Shaking incubator (30 or 37 °C, 140–220 rpm, depending on optimal growth conditions for the strain)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61h27","text":"Spectrophotometer or microplate reader (for measuring OD₆₀₀)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dtr2r","text":"Incubator (30 or 37 °C)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Take a single colony from a glycerol stock or agar plate.
Using a sterile inoculating loop, pick a small amount of the colony and inoculate it into 10 mL LB broth (in a 50 mL Falcon tube).
Gently swirl the inoculating loop to ensure the bacteria are suspended.
Loosen the cap of the culture tube (to maintain aerobic conditions) and incubate in a shaking incubator at 30 or 37 °C, 140 rpm, for 12–14 hours.
Observe whether the culture has turned turbid.
If necessary, measure OD₆₀₀ = 0.6–0.8 to confirm it is in log phase.
Prepare a fresh LB agar plate (equilibrated to room temperature).
Dip a sterile inoculating loop into the overnight culture and streak three parallel lines on the agar.
Using a new sterile loop dipped in sterile LB broth, streak three more lines at an angle, making sure the first line crosses the previous streaks.
Rotate the plate 90°, use a new sterile loop, and streak three “dry lines.”
Rotate the plate another 90° and repeat streaking three more dry lines, with the final streak extending toward the center of the plate.
Invert the plate and incubate at 30 or 37 °C for 24 hours.
On the following day, check for single colonies approximately 1–2 mm in diameter.
If growth is insufficient: extend incubation for an additional 12–24 hours.
If growth is excessive: dilute the culture before streaking (e.g., 1:4 dilution).
Using a sterile inoculating loop, carefully pick a single colony and inoculate into 10 mL LB broth.
Incubate in a shaking incubator at 30 or 37 °C, 140 rpm, for 12–14 hours.
creator: igemnthuth
